title: Runtime Secrets Management Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the runtime secrets management capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: The secret lifecycle is: put returns an opaque ref plus metadata, resolve returns the value only server-internally, rotate increments the version while keeping the same opaque ref, revoke disables resolution while retaining metadata, delete removes both metadata and the provider-held value, and readiness reports built-in and OpenBao backend health. A secret value is never returned by list or getMetadata; resolve is the single value-returning server path. Provider failure (OpenBao unreachable) reports degraded and never silently falls back to another backend.

stateModel.description: State comprises the secret ref, organisationId, name, provider in postgres|openbao, version, a revoked flag, the backend path, an encrypted_value for the built-in Postgres store or null when the value lives in OpenBao, and created, updated, and deleted metadata; list and getMetadata responses carry no value. The built-in store encrypts the value at rest while OpenBao holds the value entirely outside Postgres. Metadata is tenant-scoped under row-level security so refs do not leak across organisations.

permissions.description: Admin secret routes require platform.secrets.read or platform.secrets.write and are operator-only; there is no tenant UI surface in this pass. Tenant isolation prevents one organisation from resolving, reading, or enumerating another organisation's ref, enforced at the store layer. This is a security-sensitive capability, so permission, audit, and fail-closed behaviour must be preserved on every path.

contracts.description: The contract surface is GET and POST /api/admin/secrets, POST /api/admin/secrets/revoke, POST /api/admin/secrets/delete, and GET /api/admin/secrets/readiness, fronting a SecretStorePort with put, list, getMetadata, resolve, revoke, delete, and readiness operations behind the OpenBao and Postgres secret adapters. The port seam keeps the secret backend swappable, and resolve is deliberately the only value-returning operation. Readiness payloads carry no secret-bearing fields.

validation.description: Put returns no value, list and getMetadata are value-free, resolve is the only value-returning server path, rotation preserves the opaque ref while incrementing the version, OpenBao stores the value outside Postgres, and readiness payloads contain no secret-bearing fields. These invariants are exercised by the secret-store contract and OpenBao proofs to guarantee values never leak through metadata or readiness surfaces.

errorModel.description: Outcomes are deterministic and fail closed: a revoked, deleted, or cross-tenant ref resolves to null; an unreachable OpenBao reports degraded; an unavailable built-in Postgres degrades or skips honestly in proof; and no contract echoes a secret value on any failure path. Degraded mode disables the adapter-dependent mutation paths while keeping read-only readiness and audit visible, and no silent fallback upgrades provider readiness.

auditModel.description: put, revoke, and delete emit audit-before-change records carrying only name, ref, and provider metadata and never the value, so the audit write precedes the store mutation. Where the use-case wraps the store write, an audit failure prevents the mutation, making audit a hard precondition for change. This guarantees a complete, value-free audit trail for every secret-lifecycle action.

readinessModel.description: Readiness distinguishes built-in Postgres ready, OpenBao ready, and OpenBao degraded or unreachable, and emits secret-free readiness payloads; the built-in check is a Postgres SELECT 1 and the OpenBao check is its sys/health endpoint, and an OpenBao outage yields degraded rather than a fabricated ready. No silent fallback upgrades provider readiness, so a hermetic or composed state is never reported as a live-external-provider state.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by three runtime proofs: secret-store-contract validates the live Postgres store contract, encryption, row-level security, and audit; secrets-openbao validates the live OpenBao provider; and provider-secrets-readiness validates the ready versus degraded classification, described in the corpus as level-4 live provider and storage validation. The proofs are the lineage evidence, not a USF readiness claim. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
